---
title: "TensorToMask2D"
---

## Overview

`TensorToMask2D` decodes a single spatial channel from a model output tensor into a `Mask2D`
by collecting all pixels whose activation exceeds a configurable threshold.

## Tensor Layout

The decoder expects a **4D** tensor in **NCHW** order:

| Dim | Name    | Indexed by              |
|-----|---------|-------------------------|
| 0   | Batch   | `DecoderContext::batch_index` |
| 1   | Channel | `DecoderContext::source_channel` |
| 2   | Height  | `DecoderContext::height` |
| 3   | Width   | `DecoderContext::width` |

`DecoderContext::height` and `DecoderContext::width` must match `tensor.size(2)` and
`tensor.size(3)` respectively. `SlotAssembler` populates these from the output slot metadata
(last two entries of the slot shape).

## Coordinate Scaling

When `DecoderContext::target_image_size` is non-zero, mask coordinates are upsampled from tensor
space to the original image size using nearest-neighbor mapping. When `target_image_size` is
`{0, 0}`, coordinates are returned in tensor pixel space.

## Validation

`decode()` validates at runtime:

- Tensor is defined and 4-dimensional
- `batch_index` and `source_channel` are in range
- `height` and `width` are positive and match the tensor spatial dimensions

Invalid inputs throw `std::invalid_argument` or `std::out_of_range`, consistent with the other
channel decoders.
